Guam Congress Building

Hagåtfia, Guam 96910
Bill No. 6-38 (COR), "AN ACT TO TRANSFER LOT NO. 10063-REM-PTN-I, MUNICIPALITY
OF DEDEDO, TO SPECIAL OLYMPICS GUAM FOR THE ESTABLISHMENT OF THEIR
ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE AND TRAINING FACILITY IN SUPPORTOF GUAM'S SPECIAL
OLYMPICS ATHLETES AND PROGRAM."
Håfa Adai Mr. Speaker,
Bill 6-38 transfers Lot 17 No. 10063-REM-PTN-I from Dededo to Special Olympics Guam
("SOGU"), providing the non-profit organization with a permanent site for its Administrative Office
and Training Facility. This approximately 5,432 square meter lot will serve as a supportive
environment that fosters sportsmanship and healthy competition by providing a consistent space for
Special Olympians'training, competitions, and team-building activities.
Since its establishment in 1976, SOGU continues to embody our island's unwavering commitment to
inclusivity, providing opportunities for hundreds of local athletes with disabilities to excel in team
and competitive sports. By enacting Bill 6-38, we are honoring all the Special Olympics athletes,
coaches, staff, and volunteers who dedicate their efforts to exemplifying accessibility, respect, and
the enduring legacy ofequality and detennination that defines Special Olympics Guam.
Forthese reasons, I sign Bill No. 6-38 (COR) into law as Public Law No. 38-10.
Senseramente,
38GL-25-0731
LOURDES A. LEONGUERRERO June 18, 2025
I Maga'hågan Guåhan
8:47 a.m.

AN ACT TO TRANSFER LOT NO. 10063-REM-PTN-I,
MUNICIPALITY OF DEDEDO, TO SPECIAL OLYMPICS
GUAM FOR THE ESTABLISHMENT OF THEIR
ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE AND TRAINING FACILITY
IN SUPPORT OF GUAM'S SPECIAL OLYMPICS
ATHLETES AND PROGRAM.
1 BE IT ENACTED BY THE PEOPLE OF GUAM:
2 Section 1. Legislative Findings and Intent. ILiheslaturan Guåhan finds
3 that Guam Special Olympics is a non-profit organization established in 1976 by
4 Delrina Baza with the support of First Lady Madeleine Bordallo. That same year,
1
Doc. No. 38GL-25-0731.*

I Guam's first Special Olympics Track and Field Games were held at the John F.
2 Kennedy High School Gene Ramsey Field with onehundred fifty (150) athletes;and
3 in 2006, the organization changed its name to Special Olympics Guam (SOGU).
4 I Liheslaturan Guåhan further finds that from 1976 to 1990, the staff and
5 faculty of Chief Brodie Memorial School were instrumental in coordinating the
6 games for the Special Olympics Guam Programs. Since then, the organization has
7 grown with thousands of dedicated volunteer coordinators, trainers, coaches, event
8 staff, and cheer squads who make the year-round Special Olympics Training and
9 Games a success.
10 I Liheslaturan Guåhan further finds that since the launch of the Special
11 Olympics on Guam, over five hundred (500) athletes and unified partners have
12 participated and competed in year-round sports training, and thousands of athletes
13 have participated in games such as bowling, football (soccer), badminton, softball,
14 athletics, and aquatics; and Guam has also sent delegations to participate in four (4)
15 International/World Summer Games.
16 Therefore, it is the intent of ILiheslaturan Guåhan to transfer by grant, Lot
17 No. 10063-REM-PTN-I to the Special Olympics Guam Organization to establish
18 their administrative office and training facility in support of Guam's athletes in the
19 Special Olympics program.
20 Section 2. Transfer of Property to the Special Olympics Guam. Lot No.
21 10063-REM-PTN-I containing an area of approximately 5,432 +/- square meters
22 (58,470 +/- square feet), municipalityofDededo, as said lot is marked on Drawing
23 Number Instrument Number 00000434871 L.M., Check Number 474-FY 89, as
24 described in that Parceling Map of Lot No. 10063-REM-PTN-I at the Records
25 Division,Department ofLandManagement, Government ofGuam, underDocument
26 Number 7052, and appended to this Act as Exhibit "A," notwithstanding any other
27 provision oflaw, rule, or regulation, is herebygranted to the Special Olympics Guam
2
Doc. No. 38GL-25-0731.*

1 Organization (Grantee) for the establishment of the Special Olympics Guam
2 Administrative Office and Training Facility.
3 (a) The Property shall revert to the Chamorro Land Trust Commission
4 if the Grantee is dissolved at any time during the effect of this Act; or fails to
5 utilize the Property for the purposes prescribed in this Act, five years from
6 enactment.
7 (b) Grantee shall, in writing, indemnify the government of Guam
8 from any liability from injuries to persons or damage to property occurring on
9 the property.
10 (c) Grantee shall be responsible for all utility costs, water, power,
11 and other utility changes attributableto the property.
12 (d) Grantee shall maintain its legal nonprofit (tax exempt) status
13 pursuant the Internal Revenue Code.
14 (e) No assignment, mortgage, collateralizations, securitization,
15 encumbrance or lease may be made of the grantedproperty.
16 (f) Notwithstanding any other provision oflaw, no party shall cause
17 or record a mechanic'slien on the grantedproperty.
18 (g) The Department of Land Management shall survey or cause to
19 be surveyedLotNo. 10063-REM-PTN-I municipalityofDededo in a manner
,
20 consistent with Public Law and Guam Administrative Rules and Regulations
21 (GARR). Survey monuments shall be placed on the lot consistent with Public
22 Law and the GARR.
23 (h) The deed granting the property described in this Section shall be
24 perfectedpursuant to the provisions ofthisAct, and shall contain a revisionary
25 interest in favor of the Chamorro Land Trust Commission (Grantor).
26 Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Chamorro Land Trust
3
Doc. No. 38GL-25-0731.*

1 Commission may exercise the revisionary provisions of the deed in the event
2 that the Grantee, at any time, fails to adhere with the provisions of this Act.
3 Section 3. Effective Date. This Act shall be effective upon enactment.
4 Section 4. Severability.If any provision ofthis Act or its application to any
5 person or circumstance is found to be invalid or inorganic, such invalidity shall not
6 affect other provisions or applications of this Act that can be given effect without
7 the invalid provision or application, and to this end the provisions of this Act are
8 severable.
4
